eyebrow: Literary Palette · Sourced from setting & page
tagline: Forty-eight books, read for their colour, not their dust-jacket.
metaNote:
Color sourced from setting,
period & the printed page
sourcing: Every palette is sampled from a specific work's setting, period, and physical object — the weather and light of its world, the materials of its time, and the ink, paper, and illustration of the book itself. Iconic works are named; the aim is the colour the prose puts in your eye, not a generic 'old book' sepia.

volumes.title: The Russian Novel
volumes.eyebrow: Literary · Vol I · Sourced from Setting & Page
volumes.h1: Four palettes from the great Russian novels, snow, candlelight, and dread.
preface: The nineteenth-century Russian novel lives between the frozen white of the steppe and the gold candlelight of the salon, with the grey of Petersburg stone and the deep red of icon and uniform. Its colour is cold ground warmed only by fire and faith.
Each refuses the flag-red 'Soviet' shorthand: these are imperial palettes of snow, beeswax, and incense, not propaganda red.

palettes.kicker: War and Peace · Tolstoy · 1869 · the winter ballroom & the retreat
palettes.title: A candlelit Petersburg ball against the snow and the long retreat from Moscow
palettes.source: Color from the imperial novel: ballroom candle gold, snow white, hussar-uniform deep green, icon crimson, and the grey of a winter sky.

swatches.name: Steppe snow, blue-white
swatches.note: The frozen ground of the retreat.

d.text: Candle gold warms the imperial interiors.

s.text: Snow white, hussar green, and ash sky.

a.text: Icon crimson and gilt gold as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Soviet propaganda red. Tolstoy's Russia is candlelit gold and snow, an imperial world before the flag.

palettes.kicker: Crime and Punishment · Dostoevsky · 1866 · the Petersburg slums in July heat
palettes.title: A feverish summer in the airless garrets and yellow stairwells of Haymarket
palettes.source: Color from the fevered city: sickly stairwell yellow, grimy plaster grey, dust-brown, axe-steel cold, and a deep blood red.

d.text: Sickly yellow defines the feverish slums.

s.text: Grimy plaster, dull brown, and murky canal.

a.text: Dark blood and axe steel as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Romantic golden Petersburg. Dostoevsky's city is a sickly yellow fever-dream of poverty, not an imperial postcard.

palettes.kicker: Doctor Zhivago · Pasternak · 1957 · the ice-bound house at Varykino
palettes.title: A revolution-era winter at a frozen country house, ice-glazed and candle-warm
palettes.source: Color from the frozen idyll: ice-crystal white-blue, dim candle gold, dark fir green, wolf-grey dusk, and a flash of partisan red.

swatches.name: Candle, dim gold
swatches.note: The little warmth of the rooms.

swatches.name: Fir forest, dark green
swatches.note: The snowbound woods around the house.

d.text: Ice white-blue dominates the frozen idyll.

s.text: Candle gold, fir green, and wolf-grey dusk.

a.text: Partisan red and amber lamp as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Warm romance grade. Pasternak's idyll is cold and ice-glazed; warmth is only a candle against the freezing white.

palettes.kicker: Anna Karenina · Tolstoy · 1877 · the Moscow station in snow
palettes.title: A black-dress ball and a snowbound railway platform where the tragedy begins
palettes.source: Color from the novel: snow-platform white, Anna's black velvet, ballroom candle gold, locomotive iron, and a deep ruby.

swatches.name: Locomotive, iron grey
swatches.note: The train of the opening and the end.

swatches.name: Steam, dim silver
swatches.note: Engine steam on the cold platform.

d.text: Station snow is the cold opening field.

s.text: Black gown, candle gold, and iron train.

a.text: Ruby red and steam silver as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Warm romance gold. Anna's world is snow-white and black velvet — elegance against cold, not a soft glow.

volumes.title: Gothic & Horror
volumes.eyebrow: Literary · Vol II · Sourced from Setting & Page
volumes.h1: Four palettes from the gothic novels, moonlight, decay, and blood.
preface: Gothic fiction is built on moonlit stone, candle-shadow, mouldering velvet, and the single shock of blood. The palette is cold and dark, punctuated by fire and crimson, with the texture of decay.
Each refuses the cartoon Halloween orange-and-black: literary gothic is a subtle range of moon-grey, bruise, and oxblood.

palettes.kicker: Frankenstein · Mary Shelley · 1818 · the Arctic & the laboratory
palettes.title: A creation scene by candle and storm, framed by the white waste of the polar sea
palettes.source: Color from the creation tale: polar ice white, laboratory candle gold, storm-cloud grey, dead-flesh sallow, and electric storm blue.

swatches.name: Polar ice, cold white
swatches.note: The Arctic frame of the narration.

d.text: Polar ice white frames the whole narration.

s.text: Candle gold, slate storm, and sallow flesh.

a.text: Lightning blue and dark blood as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Lurid lab-green cliché. Shelley's horror is cold, sublime, and Arctic-white, not a comic mad-scientist set.

palettes.kicker: Dracula · Bram Stoker · 1897 · the Carpathian castle at night
palettes.title: A moonlit Transylvanian castle of black stone, candle, and the count's red
palettes.source: Color from the vampire's keep: black castle stone, moon-silver, candle amber, cobweb grey, and the deep red of cape and blood.

swatches.name: Castle stone, black
swatches.note: The dark keep swallowing the light.

swatches.name: Moonlight, cold silver
swatches.note: The blue moon on the battlements.

swatches.name: Cobweb & dust, grey
swatches.note: The decay of the old rooms.

d.text: Black castle stone is the field the moon glows on.

s.text: Silver moonlight, candle amber, and grey cobweb.

a.text: Deep cape-red and wolf-amber as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Orange-and-black Halloween. Stoker's gothic is moon-silver and oxblood on black stone, not novelty décor.

palettes.kicker: Wuthering Heights · Emily Brontë · 1847 · the storm-blown moor
palettes.title: A wild Yorkshire moor of heather, peat, and slate under a bruised sky
palettes.source: Color from the windswept moor: peat-brown ground, storm-bruise sky, dead-heather mauve, slate-grey stone, and rushlight gold.

swatches.name: Moor peat, dark brown
swatches.note: The bleak open ground of the moor.

swatches.name: Storm sky, bruise grey-violet
swatches.note: The wind-driven cloud over the heights.

d.text: Dark peat brown is the moor's dominant ground.

s.text: Bruise sky, dead heather, and slate stone.

a.text: Rushlight gold and rust bracken as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Pretty lavender moorland. Brontë's moor is bleak, bruised, and storm-dark — heather gone over, not in bloom.

palettes.kicker: Rebecca · Daphne du Maurier · 1938 · Manderley
palettes.title: A great Cornish house haunted by its dead mistress, sea-grey and blood-rhododendron
palettes.source: Color from Manderley: sea-mist grey, blood-red rhododendron, dark-panel brown, fog white, and a deep azalea pink.

swatches.name: Cornish sea-mist, grey
swatches.note: The fog over the estate and cove.

swatches.name: Rhododendron, blood red
swatches.note: The 'slaughterous red' drive of the novel.

swatches.name: Sea, cold green-grey
swatches.note: The water where the boat lies.

swatches.name: Fire, ember gold
swatches.note: The blaze that ends the house.

d.text: Sea-mist grey shrouds the whole estate.

s.text: Blood rhododendron, dark panelling, and cold sea.

a.text: Pale azalea and ember fire as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Pretty country-house pastel. Manderley is fog-grey and blood-red — menace under the gardens, not a charming manor.

volumes.title: The Modernists
volumes.eyebrow: Literary · Vol III · Sourced from Setting & Page
volumes.h1: Four palettes from the modernist city, grey stone and electric dawn.
preface: Modernist fiction maps the early-twentieth-century city — London, Dublin, New York — in its specific weather and light: Thames fog, Dublin stone, Jazz-Age glitter. The palettes are urban, atmospheric, and tuned to a single day's hours.
Each refuses the generic 'vintage' sepia: each modernist city has a precise, particular colour.

palettes.kicker: Mrs Dalloway · Virginia Woolf · 1925 · a June morning in Westminster
palettes.title: A single day in post-war London, fresh flowers and Big Ben in clear June light
palettes.source: Color from Woolf's London day: fresh flower-shop green, June sky blue, Portland-stone cream, pillarbox red, and a soft dove grey.

swatches.name: Flower-shop green, fresh
swatches.note: The morning's flowers and the parks.

swatches.name: Pillarbox, post red
swatches.note: The London red of post and bus.

d.text: Fresh flower-green leads the bright June day.

s.text: Soft sky, Portland cream, and dove grey.

a.text: Post-red and delphinium as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Foggy-grey 'old London'. Woolf's day is clear, fresh, and flowering June — luminous, not murky.

palettes.kicker: Ulysses · James Joyce · 1922 · Dublin, 16 June 1904
palettes.title: A day across Dublin, from the Martello tower's sea-green dawn to night-town
palettes.source: Color from Joyce's Dublin: Liffey grey-green, Martello sea blue, Guinness black-brown, Georgian-door colour, and gorse yellow.

swatches.name: Liffey & stone, grey-green
swatches.note: The river and the city's grey.

swatches.name: Guinness, black-brown
swatches.note: The pub's stout and dark wood.

swatches.name: Georgian door, deep red
swatches.note: The painted doors of the terraces.

swatches.name: Gorse, bright yellow
swatches.note: The flowering whin on the hill.

d.text: Grey-green stone and river are the city's body.

s.text: Sea blue-green, stout black, and door red.

a.text: Gorse yellow and brass as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Quaint emerald-Ireland green. Joyce's Dublin is grey stone, dark stout, and a snot-green sea, not a tourism shamrock.

palettes.kicker: The Great Gatsby · F. Scott Fitzgerald · 1925 · the West Egg party
palettes.title: A Jazz-Age summer night of champagne gold, the green dock light, and a yellow car
palettes.source: Color from the Jazz Age: champagne gold, the famous dock-light green, summer-night blue, cream linen, and Gatsby's yellow car.

swatches.name: Dock light, green
swatches.note: The light at the end of Daisy's dock.

d.text: Champagne gold defines the glittering parties.

s.text: Summer-night blue, cream linen, and lawn green.

a.text: The dock-light green and yellow car as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Muted period drama. The Jazz Age is gold glitter and saturated symbol-colour; restraint betrays its excess.

palettes.kicker: Heart of Darkness · Conrad · 1899 · the Congo river
palettes.title: A steamer pushing upriver into green darkness toward ivory and madness
palettes.source: Color from the river journey: oppressive jungle green, brown river, ivory cream, station-rust, and a sunset blood-orange.

swatches.name: Jungle, oppressive green
swatches.note: The wall of forest along the river.

swatches.name: Ivory, cream
swatches.note: The trade that drives the horror.

swatches.name: Steamer rust, iron brown
swatches.note: The decrepit boat and station tin.

swatches.name: Mist, sickly grey-green
swatches.note: River fog on the dawn attack.

d.text: Oppressive jungle green walls the river.

s.text: Muddy river, ivory cream, and steamer rust.

a.text: Blood-orange dusk and sickly mist as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Adventure-bright greenery. Conrad's Congo is dark, oppressive, and sickly; the green is a threat, not a paradise.

volumes.title: Magical Realism
volumes.eyebrow: Literary · Vol IV · Sourced from Setting & Page
volumes.h1: Four palettes from the magical-real, heat, dust, and impossible colour.
preface: Magical realism saturates the everyday — Latin American heat, tropical bloom, and dust given the intensity of the marvellous. The palettes are warm, earthen, and lit by one impossible hue that signals the magic seeping in.
Each refuses the flat 'exotic' brights: the colour is grounded in real heat and clay, with the marvellous as a single saturated note.

palettes.kicker: One Hundred Years of Solitude · García Márquez · 1967 · Macondo
palettes.title: A fictional Caribbean town of yellow butterflies, river stones, and tropical decay
palettes.source: Color from Macondo: dust-ochre earth, river-stone white, banana-leaf green, the famous yellow butterflies, and a deep rust of decay.

swatches.name: Macondo earth, dust ochre
swatches.note: The town's unpaved streets and clay.

swatches.name: River stones, prehistoric white
swatches.note: The 'polished like eggs' stones of the opening.

swatches.name: Banana leaf, deep green
swatches.note: The plantation closing over the town.

d.text: Dust ochre is the town's earthen field.

s.text: River-stone white, banana green, and clay wall.

a.text: Yellow butterflies and rust as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Generic tropical neon. Macondo is dust, clay, and river-stone; the magic is one note — the yellow butterflies.

palettes.kicker: The House of the Spirits · Isabel Allende · 1982 · the big house
palettes.title: A Chilean family house of green velvet, candlelight, and a clairvoyant's white
palettes.source: Color from the spirit-house: deep velvet green, candle gold, mahogany dark, Clara's spectral white, and a political flag red.

d.text: Deep velvet green furnishes the spirit-house.

s.text: Candle gold, mahogany, and cream plaster.

a.text: Spectral white and flag red as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Bright folk-art colour. Allende's house is deep, shadowed, and candlelit; the white spirit is the one luminous note.

palettes.kicker: Like Water for Chocolate · Laura Esquivel · 1989 · the ranch kitchen
palettes.title: A Mexican-revolution kitchen of chilli red, marigold, and copper pots
palettes.source: Color from the magical kitchen: chilli red, marigold orange, copper-pot warmth, masa cream, and a deep mole brown.

swatches.name: Dried chilli, deep red
swatches.note: The strings of chilli over the stove.

swatches.name: Marigold, orange-gold
swatches.note: The cempasúchil of the recipes and rites.

swatches.name: Masa & cream, pale gold
swatches.note: The dough and the table linen.

d.text: Deep chilli red leads the kitchen's palette.

s.text: Marigold, copper, and pale masa.

a.text: Dark mole and rose red as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Muted rustic browns. Esquivel's kitchen burns with chilli and marigold; the magic is heat and saturated spice.

palettes.kicker: Pedro Páramo · Juan Rulfo · 1955 · the ghost town of Comala
palettes.title: A dead Mexican village in shimmering heat where the living and dead share the dust
palettes.source: Color from Comala: heat-dust ochre, adobe ruin grey, dry-agave green, ember of an unseen fire, and a shroud white.

swatches.name: Dry agave, dust green
swatches.note: The withered plants of the plain.

swatches.name: Shadow doorway, deep brown
swatches.note: The dark of the empty rooms.

swatches.name: Shroud, bone white
swatches.note: The dead in their burial cloth.

d.text: Heat-ochre dust fills the dead town.

s.text: Adobe grey, dust-green agave, and brown shadow.

a.text: Bone shroud and dull ember as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Vivid folk colour. Comala is a bleached, dusty purgatory; its palette is heat-faded ochre, not festive brights.

volumes.title: Japanese Literature
volumes.eyebrow: Literary · Vol V · Sourced from Setting & Page
volumes.h1: Four palettes from the Japanese page, restraint, snow, and one accent.
preface: Japanese literature, from Heian court to postwar, favours restraint and the single resonant accent — indigo and ink with one note of red or gold, or the white of snow against a lacquer dark. The palettes are quiet, seasonal, and exact.
Each refuses the busy 'Japan brights' of tourism: these are spare, tonal palettes built around one deliberate colour.

palettes.kicker: The Tale of Genji · Murasaki Shikibu · c.1010 · the Heian court
palettes.title: A courtly world of layered silk robes, lacquer, and moon-viewing
palettes.source: Color from the Heian court: layered-silk plum and green, lacquer black, paper cream, gold-leaf screen, and moon silver.

swatches.name: Paper, warm cream
swatches.note: The poems exchanged on fine paper.

swatches.name: Gold screen, leaf gold
swatches.note: The byōbu screens behind the nobles.

d.text: Court-plum silk leads the layered palette.

s.text: Willow green, lacquer black, and cream paper.

a.text: Gold screen and moon silver as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Bright modern Japan. The Heian court is layered, muted silk and lacquer — subtle seasonal harmony, not neon.

palettes.kicker: Snow Country · Kawabata · 1948 · the hot-spring town in winter
palettes.title: A snowbound mountain onsen of white drift, dark cedar, and a geisha's red
palettes.source: Color from the snow country: deep snow white, cedar-dark beam, tatami straw, lamp gold, and a single obi red.

swatches.name: Snow, deep white
swatches.note: The endless snow of the mountain town.

swatches.name: Tatami, straw gold
swatches.note: The matted floors of the rooms.

swatches.name: Lamp, warm gold
swatches.note: The interior light against the snow.

swatches.name: Obi, deep red
swatches.note: Komako's sash, the one warm accent.

swatches.name: Night sky, cold indigo
swatches.note: The Milky Way over the snow.

d.text: Deep snow white dominates the winter town.

s.text: Cedar dark, straw tatami, and lamp gold.

a.text: Obi red and indigo sky as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Cosy warm palette. Kawabata's snow country is cold, white, and spare; a single red obi carries all the warmth.

palettes.kicker: Norwegian Wood · Murakami · 1987 · 1960s Tokyo & the mountain sanatorium
palettes.title: A melancholy late-sixties Tokyo of autumn green, rain-grey, and a record-sleeve red
palettes.source: Color from the novel: autumn forest green, Tokyo rain-grey, dorm-wood brown, paper-lantern cream, and a vinyl-sleeve red.

swatches.name: Record sleeve, faded red
swatches.note: The Beatles album of the title.

d.text: Autumn forest green leads the melancholy palette.

s.text: Rain grey, dorm brown, and cream lantern.

a.text: Faded record-red and dim neon as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Bright pop-sixties colour. Murakami's mood is muted, rainy, and autumnal — nostalgia in grey-green, not pop.

palettes.kicker: The Makioka Sisters · Tanizaki · 1948 · the Kyoto cherry-viewing
palettes.title: A pre-war Osaka family at the annual blossom-viewing, kimono silk against pink sakura
palettes.source: Color from the hanami: cherry-blossom pink, kimono indigo, silk-pattern gold, temple-cedar green, and a lantern red.

swatches.name: Sakura blossom, soft pink
swatches.note: The cherry trees of the yearly outing.

d.text: Soft blossom pink leads the hanami palette.

s.text: Indigo kimono, silk gold, and cedar green.

a.text: Vermilion lantern and pale stone as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Garish festival colour. Tanizaki's world is refined and seasonal — soft blossom and deep indigo, not loud spectacle.

volumes.title: Southern Gothic
volumes.eyebrow: Literary · Vol VI · Sourced from Setting & Page
volumes.h1: Four palettes from the American South, heat, dust, and faded grandeur.
preface: Southern Gothic lives in heat-hazed greens, red clay, weathered white clapboard, and the deep shade of a porch. The palette is humid, sun-faded, and overgrown, with decay always at the edge.
Each refuses the pretty plantation-romance gloss: this is a South of dust, kudzu, and peeling paint.

palettes.kicker: To Kill a Mockingbird · Harper Lee · 1960 · Maycomb in summer
palettes.title: A drowsy Alabama town in the Depression heat, red dust and deep porch shade
palettes.source: Color from Maycomb: red-clay dust, faded white clapboard, deep-shade green, courthouse-brick, and a summer-sky haze.

swatches.name: Porch shade, deep green
swatches.note: The cool dark under the eaves and oaks.

d.text: Red-clay dust is the town's dominant ground.

s.text: Faded clapboard, porch green, and dull brick.

a.text: Pale haze and magnolia cream as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Lush plantation green. Maycomb is dusty, faded, and Depression-poor — red clay and peeling paint, not a manicured estate.

palettes.kicker: A Streetcar Named Desire · Tennessee Williams · 1947 · the French Quarter flat
palettes.title: A sweaty New Orleans tenement of paper-lantern pink, humid green, and bare-bulb glare
palettes.source: Color from the Quarter flat: humid green wall, paper-lantern pink, bare-bulb white, deep shade brown, and a poker-night red.

swatches.name: Paper lantern, faded pink
swatches.note: Blanche's lantern over the bare bulb.

swatches.name: Shade, deep brown
swatches.note: The dim corners of the rooms.

d.text: Humid green is the tenement's clammy field.

s.text: Lantern pink, bare-bulb white, and deep shade.

a.text: Poker red and whiskey amber as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Romantic New Orleans glow. Williams's Quarter is sweaty, harsh, and green-lit; the lantern only hides the bare bulb.

palettes.kicker: The Sound and the Fury · Faulkner · 1929 · the Compson place
palettes.title: A decaying Mississippi estate of overgrown green, weathered grey, and honeysuckle
palettes.source: Color from the Compson decline: overgrown green, weathered-grey timber, red Mississippi clay, honeysuckle cream, and a deep shade brown.

d.text: Overgrown green smothers the failing estate.

s.text: Weathered grey, red clay, and deep shade.

a.text: Honeysuckle cream and humid sky as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Grand-mansion gloss. Faulkner's Compson place is overgrown, weathered, and declining — decay, not antebellum grandeur.

palettes.kicker: Their Eyes Were Watching God · Hurston · 1937 · the Florida muck
palettes.title: A black Florida farming town and the rich muck of the Everglades before the hurricane
palettes.source: Color from the muck: fertile black soil, bean-field green, porch-pine brown, sky-blue, and a hurricane bruise-grey.

swatches.name: Everglades muck, black soil
swatches.note: The rich dark earth of the bean fields.

swatches.name: Bean field, green
swatches.note: The crops worked on the muck.

swatches.name: Florida sky, soft blue
swatches.note: The bright sky before the storm.

d.text: Black muck soil is the fertile field.

s.text: Bean green, porch pine, and soft sky.

a.text: Storm bruise and red headwrap as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Faded sepia South. Hurston's muck is black, green, and alive — fertile and vivid, not a brown nostalgia.

volumes.title: Dystopian Fiction
volumes.eyebrow: Literary · Vol VII · Sourced from Setting & Page
volumes.h1: Four palettes from imagined tyrannies, grey states and one warning colour.
preface: Literary dystopia drains colour to signal control: institutional grey, propaganda red, and sterile white, with any warmth coming as forbidden contraband. The palette is regimented, with a single charged accent.
Each refuses a neutral 'futuristic' look: dystopia is a deliberate near-monochrome broken by the colour of the regime or the resistance.

palettes.kicker: Nineteen Eighty-Four · Orwell · 1949 · Airstrip One
palettes.title: A grimy totalitarian London of grey concrete, Victory-gin, and Party red
palettes.source: Color from Oceania: ash concrete grey, Victory-gin oily clear, Party-poster red, telescreen blue-grey, and a contraband coral.

swatches.name: Coral paperweight, warm
swatches.note: Winston's forbidden fragment of the past.

d.text: Ash concrete grey dominates the grim city.

s.text: Drab overalls, telescreen grey, and dull grime.

a.text: Party red and coral as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Sleek sci-fi chrome. Orwell's dystopia is shabby, grimy, and grey; the only colour is propaganda and one relic.

palettes.kicker: Fahrenheit 451 · Bradbury · 1953 · the fireman's city
palettes.title: A book-burning future of black ash, kerosene fire, and cold screen-blue
palettes.source: Color from the burning state: fire-orange flame, kerosene black, screen-wall blue, salamander brass, and the white of a burning page.

swatches.name: Book-fire, kerosene orange
swatches.note: The flames of the firemen's work.

swatches.name: Ash & char, black
swatches.note: The burned books and night city.

swatches.name: Burning page, white-gold
swatches.note: A page curling in the flame.

d.text: Kerosene fire-orange leads the burning palette.

s.text: Char black, cold screen-blue, and soot grey.

a.text: Brass badge and burning page as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Cool clinical future. Bradbury's world is fire and ash against screen-blue; the heat is the whole point.

palettes.kicker: The Handmaid's Tale · Atwood · 1985 · Gilead
palettes.title: A theocratic regime colour-coded by caste: handmaid red, wife green, white wings
palettes.source: Color from Gilead: handmaid red, Wife teal-green, Martha dull green, the white of a handmaid's wings, and a grey-stone wall.

swatches.name: Handmaid habit, deep red
swatches.note: The forced uniform of the handmaids.

d.text: Handmaid red is the regime's signal colour.

s.text: Wife teal, Martha green, and wall grey.

a.text: White wings and muted blue as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Random palette. Gilead is a strict colour-caste system; every hue means a role, so the scheme must be coded, not free.

palettes.kicker: The Road · Cormac McCarthy · 2006 · the ash-grey wasteland
palettes.title: A father and son crossing a burned post-apocalyptic America under perpetual ash
palettes.source: Color from the dead world: ash grey, char-black, dead-snow grey-white, dried-blood brown, and one ember of fire.

swatches.name: Burned tree, char black
swatches.note: The dead forest along the road.

swatches.name: Dead snow, grey-white
swatches.note: Grey snow under the sunless sky.

d.text: Pervasive ash grey drowns the dead world.

s.text: Char black, grey snow, and cold ruin.

a.text: Ember fire and dried blood as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Any saturation. McCarthy's world is ash and char; the single ember of fire is the only colour that survives.

volumes.title: Children's Classics & Illustration
volumes.eyebrow: Literary · Vol VIII · Sourced from Setting & Page
volumes.h1: Four palettes from the illustrated page, ink, watercolour, and wonder.
preface: Children's classics are remembered through their illustration — Tenniel's engraving, Shepard's pencil, Sendak's crosshatch, watercolour wash. The palettes come from the printed page: warm paper, ink line, and gentle colour.
Each refuses the plastic toy-brights of modern merchandise: these are the soft, printed palettes of the original books.

palettes.kicker: Alice's Adventures in Wonderland · Carroll, ill. Tenniel · 1865
palettes.title: A Victorian engraving hand-coloured: Alice blue, playing-card red, and ink line
palettes.source: Color from the classic edition: Alice's pinafore blue, card-suit red, engraving-ink black, warm page cream, and a Cheshire pink.

swatches.name: Alice's dress, pinafore blue
swatches.note: The iconic blue of the colour editions.

swatches.name: Page, warm cream
swatches.note: The aged paper of the engravings.

d.text: Pinafore blue leads the hand-coloured plate.

s.text: Cream page, ink line, and white apron.

a.text: Card red and Cheshire mauve as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Saturated cartoon Alice. The original is a hand-tinted engraving — soft blue and red on cream, with fine ink line.

palettes.kicker: Where the Wild Things Are · Sendak · 1963 · the island of the Wild Things
palettes.title: A crosshatched dream-forest of olive green, ochre beast-fur, and ink line
palettes.source: Color from Sendak's plates: forest olive green, beast-fur ochre, Max's wolf-suit cream, ink crosshatch, and a moonlit teal.

swatches.name: Dream forest, olive green
swatches.note: The watercolour jungle of the island.

swatches.name: Night sky, moonlit teal
swatches.note: The sea and sky of the voyage.

swatches.name: Beast claw & eye, amber-gold
swatches.note: The yellow eyes of the Wild Things.

d.text: Olive dream-green is the island's field.

s.text: Ochre fur, cream suit, and ink crosshatch.

a.text: Moonlit teal and amber eyes as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Bright toy-monster colour. Sendak's island is muted watercolour and ink — earthy olive and ochre, not plastic primaries.

palettes.kicker: The Little Prince · Saint-Exupéry · 1943 · the desert & the asteroid
palettes.title: A watercolour desert of pale gold sand, the prince's green coat, and a single rose red
palettes.source: Color from the author's own watercolours: desert pale gold, prince-coat green, scarf-yellow, star-night blue, and the rose's red.

swatches.name: Sahara sand, pale gold
swatches.note: The watercolour desert of the crash.

d.text: Pale gold sand is the desert's field.

s.text: Soft green coat, warm scarf, and star-night blue.

a.text: The rose red and golden hair as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Bold graphic colour. Saint-Exupéry's own art is delicate watercolour — pale washes, with the rose as the one true red.

palettes.kicker: Winnie-the-Pooh · Milne, ill. Shepard · 1926 · the Hundred Acre Wood
palettes.title: A gentle pencil-and-wash wood of honey gold, soft green, and warm paper
palettes.source: Color from Shepard's plates: honey gold, soft-wash green, warm page cream, pencil-line grey, and a balloon red.

d.text: Honey gold leads the gentle wood.

s.text: Soft-green wash, cream page, and pencil grey.

a.text: Faded balloon red and pale sky as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Bright Disney primaries. Shepard's wood is delicate pencil and pale wash — honey and soft green on cream, not bold colour.

volumes.title: Beat & Mid-Century America
volumes.eyebrow: Literary · Vol IX · Sourced from Setting & Page
volumes.h1: Four palettes from mid-century America, highway, neon, and grain.
preface: The Beats and their mid-century contemporaries wrote a America of desert highway, diner neon, and tobacco-stained interiors — warm, restless, and a little worn. The palettes are road-dusty and lamplit.
Each refuses the clean retro-Americana of nostalgia branding: this is the grainy, smoke-stained version, not the diner-mug pastiche.

palettes.kicker: On the Road · Kerouac · 1957 · the cross-country highway at dusk
palettes.title: A westward highway at sundown, desert gold, neon-motel red, and asphalt grey
palettes.source: Color from the road: desert gold light, asphalt grey, motel-neon red, sky-blue dusk, and a tobacco amber.

swatches.name: Desert dusk, gold light
swatches.note: The low western sun on the plains.

swatches.name: Diner & car, warm chrome cream
swatches.note: The roadside stops and old cars.

swatches.name: Tobacco, amber brown
swatches.note: Cigarettes and whiskey on the move.

d.text: Desert gold light leads the highway palette.

s.text: Asphalt grey, fading sky, and chrome cream.

a.text: Motel neon and tobacco amber as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Clean retro-diner pastel. Kerouac's road is dusty, warm, and worn — gold light and grain, not a mint-and-chrome ad.

palettes.kicker: The Bell Jar · Sylvia Plath · 1963 · New York & the suburb
palettes.title: A summer of glossy magazine colour curdling into clinical white and shadow
palettes.source: Color from the descent: magazine glossy red, hospital white, suburban-lawn green, shadow grey, and a patent-black.

swatches.name: Magazine red, glossy
swatches.note: The fashion-world red of the opening.

swatches.name: Patent shoe, black
swatches.note: The glossy accessories of the magazine job.

d.text: Clinical white dominates the novel's second half.

s.text: Glossy red, lawn green, and cool shadow.

a.text: Patent black and pale pill as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Cheerful sixties palette. Plath's colour curdles from glossy red into clinical white — brightness turning sterile.

palettes.kicker: The Catcher in the Rye · Salinger · 1951 · winter New York
palettes.title: A cold Manhattan wander of grey slush, hunting-cap red, and hotel-lobby gold
palettes.source: Color from Holden's days: winter grey city, hunting-cap red, hotel-lobby gold, Central-Park pond green-black, and a taxi yellow.

swatches.name: Park pond, green-black
swatches.note: The frozen lagoon and its ducks.

d.text: Cold grey city is the winter field.

s.text: Hunting-cap red, lobby gold, and drab coat.

a.text: Pond green-black and cab yellow as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Warm nostalgic New York. Holden's city is cold, grey, and alienating; the red cap is his one note of self.

palettes.kicker: Death of a Salesman · Arthur Miller · 1949 · the Loman house
palettes.title: A cramped 1940s Brooklyn house hemmed by apartment blocks, with bright stocking-flashbacks
palettes.source: Color from the play: drab house brown, looming brick red, kitchen-linoleum grey, flashback-leaf green, and stocking nylon tan.

swatches.name: Flashback leaf, green
swatches.note: The remembered elms, brighter and gone.

d.text: Drab house brown defines the cramped home.

s.text: Looming brick, cold lino, and flashback green.

a.text: Stocking tan and dull gold as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Warm nostalgic Americana. Miller's home is drab and hemmed-in; brightness lives only in doomed memory.

volumes.title: Epic Fantasy
volumes.eyebrow: Literary · Vol X · Sourced from Setting & Page
volumes.h1: Four palettes from invented worlds, mapped in earth and weather.
preface: Epic fantasy builds its worlds in elemental colour — the green Shire, the grey of a northern wall, the gold of an old empire. The palettes are landscape-led, each realm a distinct climate and material culture.
Each refuses the generic 'medieval' brown-grey murk: each invented land has a specific, grounded palette.

palettes.kicker: The Lord of the Rings · Tolkien · 1954 · the Shire in late summer
palettes.title: A green hobbit country of round doors, gold fields, and warm hearth-light
palettes.source: Color from the Shire: rolling hill green, harvest gold, round-door colour, brick-and-thatch, and a hearth-fire amber.

swatches.name: Hobbiton hills, rolling green
swatches.note: The lush green of the Shire.

swatches.name: Hearth fire, amber
swatches.note: The warmth of the smial within.

d.text: Rolling hill green is the Shire's body.

s.text: Harvest gold, door green, and warm thatch.

a.text: Hearth amber and party gold as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Grim medieval murk. The Shire is lush, warm, and pastoral — green and gold, not the grey of a war-torn realm.

palettes.kicker: A Game of Thrones · Martin · 1996 · Winterfell & the Wall
palettes.title: A grey northern stronghold of stone, snow, and the cold green of the godswood
palettes.source: Color from the North: castle grey stone, snow white, godswood blood-leaf red, dark direwolf, and a hearth gold.

swatches.name: Direwolf & fur, dark grey
swatches.note: The Stark sigil and the cold wardrobe.

swatches.name: Weirwood leaf, blood red
swatches.note: The red five-pointed leaves and face.

d.text: Grey castle stone is the North's body.

s.text: Snow white, bone weirwood, and dark fur.

a.text: Blood-red leaf and hearth gold as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Warm high-fantasy gold. The North is grey, white, and cold; the only saturation is the weirwood's blood-red leaf.

palettes.kicker: A Wizard of Earthsea · Le Guin · 1968 · the island archipelago
palettes.title: A sea-world of slate water, fishing-village ochre, and a dragon's bronze
palettes.source: Color from the archipelago: deep sea slate-blue, weathered-wood ochre, sail brown, dragon bronze, and a mage-fire blue-white.

swatches.name: Archipelago sea, slate blue
swatches.note: The cold water between the isles.

d.text: Slate-blue sea is the archipelago's field.

s.text: Ochre wood, hemp sail, and grey stone.

a.text: Dragon bronze and mage-fire as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Bright fantasy spectacle. Earthsea is a sober sea-world of slate and ochre; magic is one cold blue-white light.

palettes.kicker: Mistborn · Brandon Sanderson · 2006 · the ash-fall Final Empire
palettes.title: A tyrant's empire under perpetual ashfall, brown mists, and noble jewel-tones
palettes.source: Color from the Final Empire: falling-ash grey, brown night-mist, skaa-rags brown, noble jewel-blue, and the red of the bloody sun.

swatches.name: Ashfall, grey
swatches.note: The ash drifting endlessly from the sky.

swatches.name: Night mist, brown-grey
swatches.note: The mists that rule the dark.

swatches.name: Plant life, ash-dulled green
swatches.note: The brown-green of a sunless world.

d.text: Falling ash grey covers the whole empire.

s.text: Brown mist, skaa rags, and dulled green.

a.text: Jewel-blue gown and bloody sun as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Bright high-fantasy colour. The Final Empire is ash and brown mist; the noble jewel-tones are a scarce mark of rank.

volumes.title: Latin & Mediterranean
volumes.eyebrow: Literary · Vol XI · Sourced from Setting & Page
volumes.h1: Four palettes from the southern sun, stucco, sea, and dust.
preface: The literature of Spain, Italy, and the Mediterranean is lit by hard southern sun on stucco, terracotta, and dust, with the deep blue of the sea and the black of mourning dress. The palettes are warm, faded, and sun-struck.
Each refuses the tourism-poster cobalt-and-white: these are lived-in southern palettes of dust, ochre, and shade.

palettes.kicker: Don Quixote · Cervantes · 1605 · the plains of La Mancha
palettes.title: A sun-bleached Castilian plain of dust, windmill stone, and a rusted suit of armour
palettes.source: Color from La Mancha: bleached dust ochre, windmill-stone grey, dry-wheat gold, armour rust, and a hard blue sky.

swatches.name: Wine & cloak, deep red
swatches.note: The inn's wine and a traveller's cloak.

d.text: Dust ochre is the bleached plain's field.

s.text: Windmill grey, dry-wheat gold, and hard sky.

a.text: Armour rust and wine red as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Lush romantic Spain. La Mancha is arid, pale, and dusty — bleached ochre and stone, not a verdant idyll.

palettes.kicker: The Leopard · Lampedusa · 1958 · Sicily in the 1860s
palettes.title: A fading aristocratic Sicily of sun-scorched gold, palazzo fresco, and dust
palettes.source: Color from the Risorgimento estate: scorched gold hills, faded fresco rose, palazzo gilt, dust grey, and a ballroom crimson.

d.text: Scorched gold is the Sicilian estate's field.

s.text: Faded fresco, antique gilt, and pale dust.

a.text: Ball crimson and pistachio as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Bright Mediterranean cliché. Lampedusa's Sicily is faded, golden, and dying — antique splendour in dust, not postcard blue.

palettes.kicker: My Brilliant Friend · Ferrante · 2011 · a poor Naples neighbourhood
palettes.title: A 1950s Neapolitan rione of grey tenement, washing-line white, and Vesuvian haze
palettes.source: Color from the rione: grey tenement concrete, washing-line white, faded shutter green, dust-ochre courtyard, and a flash of festival red.

swatches.name: Vesuvius haze, dusty violet
swatches.note: The volcano on the hazed horizon.

d.text: Grey tenement concrete is the rione's field.

s.text: Washing white, faded shutter, and ochre dust.

a.text: Festa red and Vesuvian haze as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Picturesque Amalfi colour. Ferrante's Naples is a poor grey rione of concrete and laundry, not a pastel coastline.

palettes.kicker: Love in the Time of Cholera · García Márquez · 1985 · the Caribbean port
palettes.title: A faded colonial river-port of pastel stucco, riverboat, and tropical decay
palettes.source: Color from the port city: faded-stucco rose, riverboat white, Magdalena-river brown, balcony green, and a bougainvillea magenta.

swatches.name: Cholera flag, yellow
swatches.note: The quarantine signal on the boat.

d.text: Faded stucco rose is the port's field.

s.text: Peeling boat white, river brown, and green shutters.

a.text: Bougainvillea magenta and cholera yellow as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Bright tropical resort colour. The port is faded, colonial, and decaying — sun-bleached pastel, not a vivid beach town.

volumes.title: Victorian London
volumes.eyebrow: Literary · Vol XII · Sourced from Setting & Page
volumes.h1: Four palettes from the Victorian city, fog, gaslight, and soot.
preface: The Victorian novel's London is fog, soot, gaslight, and the deep colours of a respectable interior — bottle green, oxblood, and brass against perpetual grey. The palette is dim, warm-lamped, and coal-stained.
Each refuses the cheery 'Dickensian Christmas-card' brights: this London is a sooty, gaslit grey warmed only by fire and lamp.

palettes.kicker: Bleak House · Dickens · 1853 · a November fog over the city
palettes.title: London fog so thick the gaslamps blur, mud in the streets and Chancery dust
palettes.source: Color from the fog: pervasive fog grey-brown, gaslamp halo yellow, river mud, soot black, and a legal-wax red.

d.text: Grey-brown fog smothers the whole city.

s.text: Gaslamp yellow, river mud, and soot black.

a.text: Sealing-wax red and dull brass as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Cheery Christmas-card London. Dickens's fog is a choking grey-brown; the warmth is one gaslamp blurred in murk.

palettes.kicker: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes · Doyle · 1892 · 221B Baker Street
palettes.title: A gaslit Baker Street study of bottle green, fire-amber, and Persian-slipper red
palettes.source: Color from the sitting room: bottle-green walls, fire amber, tobacco brown, gaslight gold, and a Turkey-carpet red.

swatches.name: Study wall, bottle green
swatches.note: The deep green of the 221B sitting room.

swatches.name: Gaslight, dim gold
swatches.note: The lamps of the foggy street.

d.text: Bottle-green walls define the study.

s.text: Fire amber, tobacco brown, and dim gaslight.

a.text: Turkey-carpet red and brass as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Bright modern study. Holmes's rooms are dim, green, and gaslit — warm clutter in shadow, not a clean set.

palettes.kicker: The Picture of Dorian Gray · Wilde · 1890 · the aesthete's drawing room
palettes.title: A decadent aesthete's interior of peacock blue, gilt, opium-den shadow, and yellow book
palettes.source: Color from the decadent rooms: peacock blue-green, gilt gold, opium-shadow plum, the 'poisonous yellow book', and a hothouse-orchid mauve.

swatches.name: Gilt frame, gold
swatches.note: The portrait's frame and the fittings.

swatches.name: Opium shadow, deep plum
swatches.note: The dens and the corrupted dark.

swatches.name: Hothouse orchid, mauve
swatches.note: The exotic blooms of the aesthete.

d.text: Peacock blue-green leads the decadent interior.

s.text: Gilt gold, opium plum, and warm panelling.

a.text: The yellow book and orchid mauve as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Restrained period grey. Wilde's aesthete drowns in saturated peacock, gilt, and plum — decadence, not sobriety.

palettes.kicker: Strange Case of Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde · Stevenson · 1886 · foggy Soho
palettes.title: A respectable London laced with fog and a back-door laboratory of chemical light
palettes.source: Color from the double life: Soho fog brown, gaslight halo, mahogany respectability, chemical-green tincture, and a deep door-red.

swatches.name: Soho fog, dim brown-grey
swatches.note: The murk of the disreputable streets.

d.text: Dim fog brown-grey fills the Soho streets.

s.text: Gaslight gold, mahogany, and soot brick.

a.text: Chemical-green tincture and blistered door as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Clean Victorian study. Stevenson's London is fog-brown and gaslit; the one unnatural colour is the chemical-green potion.
